ddls:
CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of the device (sensor, actuator, etc.) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the device in the facility */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status of the device */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the device */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the device */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the device was installed */,
  "warranty_expiration" TEXT /* Date the warranty expires */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "next_maintenance_due" TEXT /* Date the next maintenance is due */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_parameters" (
  "param_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device this parameter is associated with */,
  "param_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter (e.g., I32INP, I32OUT) */,
  "param_value" TEXT /* Current value of the parameter */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the parameter (e.g., DBF_LONG, DBF_ULONG) */,
  "min_value" TEXT /* Minimum value the parameter can take */,
  "max_value" TEXT /* Maximum value the parameter can take */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the parameter */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the parameter */,
  "editable" INTEGER /* Whether the parameter is editable */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the parameter */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last modification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("param_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_parameters_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_types" (
  "data_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data type */,
  "data_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the data type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data type */,
  "min_value" TEXT /* Minimum value the data type can represent */,
  "max_value" TEXT /* Maximum value the data type can represent */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the data type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_levels" (
  "access_level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access level */,
  "level_name" TEXT /* Name of the access level (e.g., Read, Write, Admin) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the access level */,
  "can_edit" INTEGER /* Whether users with this access level can edit parameters */,
  "can_delete" INTEGER /* Whether users with this access level can delete parameters */,
  "can_view_logs" INTEGER /* Whether users with this access level can view logs */,
  "can_view_alerts" INTEGER /* Whether users with this access level can view alerts */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., Technician, Engineer) */,
  "access_level_id" INTEGER /* Associated access level for the role */,
  "can_view_devices" INTEGER /* Whether users with this role can view devices */,
  "can_view_parameters" INTEGER /* Whether users with this role can view parameters */,
  "can_view_configs" INTEGER /* Whether users with this role can view configurations */,
  "can_view_logs" INTEGER /* Whether users with this role can view logs */,
  "can_view_alerts" INTEGER /* Whether users with this role can view alerts */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_access_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("access_level_id") REFERENCES access_levels ("access_level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for secure authentication */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Role assigned to the user */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device associated with this log */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the log entry was created */,
  "operation" TEXT /* Type of operation performed (e.g., Update, Read) */,
  "change_details" TEXT /* Details of the changes made to the device parameters */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the operation */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the operation was performed */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., Update, Read, Write) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_logs_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., Warning, Critical) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Alert message providing details about the issue */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was triggered */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who was notified about the alert */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Whether the alert has been resolved */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes about how the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_types" (
  "device_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the device type (e.g., Sensor, Actuator) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the device type */,
  "compatible_with" TEXT /* A list of device types that are compatible with this type */,
  "recommended_maintenance" TEXT /* Recommended maintenance procedures for this type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device this configuration belongs to */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration setting */,
  "config_value" TEXT /* Current value of the configuration */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the configuration was last modified */,
  "min_value" TEXT /* Minimum value the configuration can take */,
  "max_value" TEXT /* Maximum value the configuration can take */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the configuration */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the configuration */,
  "editable" INTEGER /* Whether the configuration is editable */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the configuration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_last_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("last_modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_parameter_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access entry */,
  "param_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parameter for which access is controlled */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role that has access to this parameter */,
  "can_view" INTEGER /* Whether the role can view the parameter */,
  "can_edit" INTEGER /* Whether the role can edit the parameter */,
  "can_delete" INTEGER /* Whether the role can delete the parameter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_parameter_access_param_id FOREIGN KEY ("param_id") REFERENCES device_parameters ("param_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_parameter_access_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)
